title: Sergio Aragonés Stomps Star Wars

'''''Sergio Aragonés Stomps Star Wars''''' is a one-shot humor comic book published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. It parodies events from movie episodes [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|I]], [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|IV]], [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|V]], and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|VI]]. It is not considered [[canon]]ical.

{{Comics-stub}}

==Plot summary==
[[Sergio Aragonés]] and [[Mark Evanier]] go to [[Lucasfilm]], where they have an appointment with [[Lucy Autrey Wilson|Lucy Wilson]], about making a ''[[Star Wars]]'' comic for [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]]. Aragonés is a delusioned ''Star Wars'' fan, who always carries a toy [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and asks the Lucasfilm employees about meeting characters such as [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. During Aragonés and Evanier's visit, the original Star Wars trilogy reels are being digitized for modern projectors. While Aragonés is looking at the digitizing equipment, an independent filmmaker (who entered the building disguised as [[George Lucas]]) sabotages the electricity while installing a bomb and causes Aragonés to be transferred into [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].

Aragonés then arrives at the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel base]] in [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where he is mistaken for [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]]. After meeting [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] (“You are prettier than the action figures! And taller too!”), he goes to a rendezvous where the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Death Star attack]] is being arranged. Aragonés at first is afraid and tries to escape, but his lifelong crush, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], convinces him otherwise. While checking the digitized movie, Evanier recognizes Aragonés among the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] pilots, and after Aragonés' [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] is destroyed, Evanier tells the projector staff to shift to another scene before his friend dies.

The scene shifts to [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]], where Aragonés talks to [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] before getting picked up by the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] take Aragonés to [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], and the trio jumps off the ship as they notice a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] is attracting them to [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. Han and Chewie are shot by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] who proceeds to attack Aragonés. After the ghost of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] contacts Aragonés to use the “Farse” (“Not '[[The Force/Legends|The Force]]'! You can't use the Force! It has elevated standards!”), he proceeds to use a pencil to draw funny pictures of Vader, which embarrasses the Sith and causes him to collapse. Unfortunately, this happens just as George Lucas himself is showing the digitized movie to international distributors, who proceed to complain about the film being ridiculous and get away. Security then beats Lucas as he is mistaken for the impostor.

The digitizing experts decide to download Aragonés out of the film, just as Leia is about to kiss the cartoonist during a medal ceremony. Security then notices the saboteur, who reveals a remote control, which if pressed will trigger the explosives set around Lucasfilm. Aragonés materializes and tackles the saboteur, who is then taken by security. Aragonés picks up the remote and accidentally presses the button, causing the entire building to blow up. Aragonés and Evanier are fired from Dark Horse (“I asked when we could work for them again. They said something about 'when Hell freezes over'.”), and George Lucas is forced to beg for money in the streets.

==Continuity==
Among the Lucasfilm and affiliates employees who appear are [[John Knoll]], [[Rob Coleman]], [[Scott Squires]] and [[Dennis Muren]] (visual effects artists), [[Rick McCallum]] (producer), [[Howard Roffman]] (vice-president), and [[Jim Morris]] (president of [[Industrial Light & Magic|Lucas Digital]]).

In a reference to other movies with effects by [[Industrial Light & Magic]], Scott Squires wears a [[Wikipedia:Roger Rabbit|Roger Rabbit]] shirt, and a doll of [[Wikipedia:The Mask|The Mask]] (who originated in comics by Dark Horse) is seen at the effects artists' table.

A group of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] attacks the saboteur in his first attempt to invade Lucasfilm.

Evanier mentions that when visiting [[Steven Spielberg]], Aragonés made a mountain out of mashed potatoes - a reference to Spielberg's [[Wikipedia:Close Encounters of the Third Kind|''Close Encounters of the Third Kind'']].

At the newsstand of the last page, a comic book starring [[Wikipedia:Groo the Wanderer|Groo]], an Aragonés character, can be seen.
